Event_Macro: China devalues the yuan three times in the same week. On Aug-24, the Shanghai Stock Exchange falls 8% in a single session and the so-called "Black Monday of 2015" spreads across global markets.
Breakdown_Micro: China accounted for over 80% of global BTC volume at the time. With the Chinese economy collapsing, Bitcoin was one of the first assets to be liquidated. On Aug-19, Bitfinex registers a 29% flash crash in 30 minutes due to cascading leveraged liquidations. BTC drops from US$ 265 to US$ 210 in ten days, the largest fall of the year.

Event_Macro: Phase of digesting the October gains. Price held consistently above US$ 320–350. On Nov-26, global volume hits 2.86M BTC in a single day — the largest record in history at the time.
Breakdown_Micro: Alistair Milne (Altana Digital Currency Fund) told CoinDesk that most of the volume was speculative, amplified by the recent introduction of margin trading on exchanges. The old resistances of US$ 320 and US$ 350 became support.
